title: I/O function calls should not be vulnerable to path injection attacks
description: Path injection vulnerabilities occur when an application uses untrusted user input to construct file paths for I/O operations without proper validation. Attackers can inject malicious path components like '../', absolute paths, or special characters to access files outside the intended directory, potentially leading to unauthorized file access, data disclosure, or system compromise. This rule detects I/O function calls that may be vulnerable to such attacks.
recommendation: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.

compliant: const safePath = path.resolve('./uploads', path.basename(fileName)); fs.readFile(safePath, callback);

checks.message: I/O function call may be vulnerable to path injection attacks
File operation uses potentially unsafe path construction
